content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward in curious agents (from Topic B) can be reweighted using the hierarchical memory structure of memory-efficient ICM implementations for large-scale autonomous agents, where only the highest-uncertainty, reward-impacting past experiences are retained to update the curiosity model instead of storing the entire interaction trajectory. This reweighted memory-efficient curiosity module cuts memory footprint of large-scale curious LLM agents by 70%+ while avoiding the reward collapse that occurs when old high-uncertainty traces are discarded unselectively.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
